stable_record_id_method: Hansard: statement-hansard-<lowercase ContributionExtId>-s<zero-padded sentence index>-o001. First-party pages: statement-<fixed source key>-o<zero-padded occurrence ordinal>. Record IDs identify source occurrence, not semantic topic; exact duplicate IDs are rejected.

source_inventory.notes: Accepted pre-existing fixed official corpus. Candidate scan used only literal first-person/collective future forms and explicit first-person position verbs.

source_inventory.title: Assisted Dying Statement
source_inventory.captured_text: I wanted to take the time to explain my opposition to this legislation. I am not satisfied that this legislation provides these safeguards – and that is why I cannot support it. I would welcome an independent review on the existing law and a public consultation on proposals for change.

source_inventory.title: Christmas Newsletter – 2024
source_inventory.captured_text: I will continue to speak up for rent controls and for a huge council-house-building program. The Archway Campus development site is a hugely contentious issue, and along with many other constituents I strongly oppose the current proposal, and have made this clear to the Council. I will continue to campaign for wealth taxes so we can invest in a more equal and sustainable future. We will continue to call on our government to end all arms sales to Israel. I will continue to speak up for the rights of Kurdish people, and oppose Israel’s illegal occupation of the Golan Heights.

source_inventory.title: Ofwat deal statement
source_inventory.captured_text: We need a system that puts the environment and public health first, and that means ending the failed experiment of water privatisation once and for all. I will continue to work toward this aim.

source_inventory.title: Statement on Gaza ceasefire
source_inventory.captured_text: Peace & Justice Project will continue to demand accountability for those responsible for genocide and crimes against humanity in Gaza. We call on the British government to cooperate fully with the International Criminal Courts as they pursue justice against all those who have committed war crimes. We will continue to stand in solidarity with the Palestinian people until they can live in peace, dignity and freedom.

source_inventory.title: Jeremy Corbyn announces bill to launch inquiry into British involvement in Gaza genocide
source_inventory.captured_text: The British government is complicit in one of the greatest crimes of our time. We will not give up until we have justice for the people of Palestine.

source_inventory.title: Jeremy Corbyn on the proscription of Palestine Action
source_inventory.captured_text: The government's proposal to proscribe Palestine Action as a terrorist organisation is as absurd as it is authoritarian. We will keep campaigning for an end to military cooperation with Israel, and we will not rest until we have brought about the only path to peace: freedom and justice for the Palestinian people.

source_inventory.title: Jeremy Corbyn announces The Gaza Tribunal
source_inventory.captured_text: That is why we are holding The Gaza Tribunal. We will uncover the truth - and we will bring about justice for the Palestinian people.

candidate_method.review_rule: Every gated occurrence was reviewed in adjacent sentence context. Procedural futures, hopes, predictions, hypothetical quotations and non-speaker quotations were excluded rather than promoted.
